whatThisIs: Show adult laboratory reference ranges drawn from the NIH MedlinePlus reference set: CBC (WBC, hemoglobin, hematocrit, platelets, MCV, MCH, MCHC, RDW), basic metabolic panel (Na, K, Cl, HCO3, BUN, creatinine, glucose, calcium), liver panel (AST, ALT, ALP, total/direct bilirubin, albumin, total protein), coagulation (PT/INR, aPTT, fibrinogen), thyroid (TSH, free T4, free T3), and the common cardiac, lipid, and inflammatory markers. Sophie Well displays the lab name, the conventional-units range, and the SI-units range side by side.
whenToUse: Use this when interpreting a lab result a patient or learner is asking about, when teaching the difference between an institutional range and the broad reference range, or when prepping a quick reference card for a new rotation. Reference ranges vary by lab and assay - the institutional range printed on the actual report is the one that defines normal for that draw; this tool is the population-level reference that a clinician can use to teach or to triage values when the local range is unavailable.
